payload.title: Spike: sync-cursor protocol for offline-first clients
payload.description:
OPEN QUESTION: What is the wire protocol that lets the CLI and iOS app keep their LOCAL event logs and reconcile with the cloud replica? This is where the de-collision work cashes out.

Shape to design:
- Pull: 'give me every event since cursor X' (what is the cursor — a ULID high-water mark? a per-tenant monotonic sequence? a vector clock?).
- Push: 'here are my N offline events' — server appends to the canonical union, runs replay + SCP-110 de-collision, returns any display-number reassignment notices.
- The hard case to get right: a client offline for a week uploads 400 events that interleave (by canonical ts,id order) with events the server accepted from other clients meanwhile. Result must match what local file-union replay would have produced.

Questions to resolve:
- Cursor semantics + how a fresh client bootstraps (full log download vs snapshot + tail).
- Idempotency: re-uploading the same ULID event must be a no-op (events are content-addressed by ULID — confirm).
- Ordering guarantees the client can rely on for optimistic UI.
- How display-number reassignment notices propagate back to a client that already showed SCP-42 locally.
- Bandwidth: delta/snapshot strategy for large logs (relates to [[106]]).

Deliverable: comment with a proposed protocol spec (endpoints + cursor format + reconciliation algorithm). Likely spawns: 'Implement /sync pull+push endpoints', 'iOS sync engine', 'CLI sync command', and an ADR.
